doctor_description: Peribronchovascular micronodules up to 3mm images 177-194
validator_description: Centrilobular micronodules up to 3mm images 177-194 — tree-in-bud pattern

doctor_description: Solid subpleural nodule 2.5×1.6cm irregular margins and spiculation — suspicious neoplastic; PET/CT or biopsy
validator_description: Solid subpleural nodule 2.5×1.6cm irregular margins and spiculation — suspicious neoplastic; PET/CT or biopsy

notes: Both identify dominant suspicious RLL nodule with same management recommendation.

validator_description: Solid nodule 3mm image 133

validator_description: Centrilobular nodules up to 7mm images 136-154 — tree-in-bud pattern
management_impact: None — both interpreted as bronchiolitis; management concordant
notes: Doctor splits into micronodule cluster and separate 8mm nodule; validator unifies as tree-in-bud up to 7mm. Minor size discrepancy (8mm vs 7mm) and pattern characterization difference.

doctor_description: Confluent centrilobular emphysema
validator_description: Severe confluent centrilobular emphysema
management_impact: None — severity label difference, no different management trigger

notes: Doctor omits bronchial secretion context. Validator identifies secretion contributing to lingular atelectasis.

notes: Doctor omits small hiatal hernia. No management change.

notes: Doctor omits fatty myocardial inclusions. Clinically relevant finding indicating prior ischemic injury.

management_impact: None from CT alone
notes: Doctor omits valve calcifications. Both valves identified by validator.

validator_description: Cardiomegaly CTR 0.57; mild pericardial effusion 8mm; severe CAC + PCP referral

anatomical_location: Right scapula, T6, T7, T10, right 8th rib
validator_description: Bone islands right scapula, T6, T7, T10, and right 8th rib — clinically insignificant
management_impact: None — clinically insignificant
notes: Doctor omits multiple bone islands. No management consequence.

validator_description: Right adrenal lesion 1.0cm 18 HU — follow-up adrenal protocol CT in 12 months recommended; consider biochemical evaluation to exclude pheochromocytoma
management_impact: Doctor completely misses right adrenal lesion requiring 12-month dedicated follow-up CT and biochemical evaluation. Management change is definitive.
notes: Critical miss: right adrenal 1.0cm at 18 HU is indeterminate and requires follow-up per ACR White Paper. Doctor mentions only left adrenal thickening and misses the right adrenal entirely.

doctor_description: Left adrenal gland thickened up to 11mm — impression: 'thickening left adrenal gland' (no management stated)
validator_description: Left adrenal lesion 1.1cm 7 HU — benign lesion, no imaging follow-up required
management_impact: Doctor fails to characterize as benign and fails to explicitly state no follow-up — Measure 405 not satisfied. Vague 'thickening' without management implies possible workup.
notes: Doctor describes left adrenal as 'thickened' without characterization or management. Validator correctly identifies as benign lesion at 7 HU and closes with no follow-up per Measure 405.

doctor_description: Three lesions (25 HU/76 HU/21 HU) — CT or MRI recommended for 2 indeterminate; no further evaluation for 3rd
validator_description: Same three lesions with identical management recommendations
management_impact: None
notes: Fully concordant.

validator_description: Hypodense lesion 2 HU, 1.0cm — follow-up at 2 and 4 years

validator_description: Mild degenerative changes thoracic spine + osteophytes

validator_description: Calculus 3mm definitively
management_impact: None — same clinical management

finding_category: RLL consolidation — characterization and growth assessment
doctor_description: Subpleural focal consolidation 2.5×1.3cm (prev 2.4×1.3cm) — essentially stable; may be rounded atelectasis; CT follow-up 6-12 months
validator_description: Subpleural area of consolidation 2.2×1.1cm (prev 1.6×1.0cm) — interval growth; concern for possible neoplastic etiology; inflammatory less likely; CT follow-up
management_impact: Both recommend CT follow-up; management equivalent. Validator's neoplastic framing may appropriately increase urgency.
notes: Size measurements differ (2.5×1.3cm vs 2.2×1.1cm) and interval comparison differs (stable vs growing). Validator's neoplastic framing vs doctor's atelectasis framing represent clinically distinct characterizations.

validator_description: Cavitating nodules up to 6mm images 131, 137, 143 — unchanged; may be pseudocavities
management_impact: None — stable cavitating/pseudocavitating nodules in context of chronic lung disease
notes: Doctor describes overall parenchymal changes but does not specifically identify cavitating nodules. Unchanged status limits clinical urgency.

validator_description: Reticular (fibrous) changes with traction bronchioloectasis dorsal parts both lungs — unchanged
management_impact: None — stable interstitial pattern on chronic follow-up
notes: Doctor omits the interstitial fibrotic pattern with traction bronchioloectasis that validator identifies. Stable finding.

doctor_description: Left adrenal lateral limb nodule -5 HU, 9×9mm — per ACR White Paper 2017 follow-up not required
management_impact: None — doctor correctly manages benign lipid-rich adenoma with no follow-up; validator omits
notes: Doctor documents and correctly applies Measure 405 (no follow-up for benign adrenal at -5 HU). Validator omits this finding. Doctor is more thorough.

validator_description: Mild calcifications aortic and mitral valves
management_impact: None from CT alone
notes: Doctor notes aortic valve calcification in impression but omits mitral valve calcification. Validator notes both.

validator_description: Multiple stable hypodense fluid-density lesions — per ACR White Paper no follow-up

validator_description: RML 3mm, RLL 4mm cavitating, LUL 4mm, LLL 6mm — new; consistent with coccidioidomycosis

doctor_description: Pulmonary markings heterogeneously increased — thickening intralobular/interlobular septa; bilateral cysts and bronchiectasis
validator_description: Bilateral diffuse subpleural GGOs, reticulations, traction bronchiectasis — stable ILD (NSIP vs UIP)
management_impact: None — both reach same conclusion (possible UIP/NSIP); management unchanged
notes: Validator synthesizes interstitial pattern more explicitly as stable ILD with NSIP/UIP differential. Doctor describes component findings without ILD synthesis.

management_impact: None from CT alone — important cardiac finding suggesting chronic disease
notes: Doctor states no cardiomegaly; validator identifies left cardiac chamber enlargement + LV calcifications. Clinically relevant omission.

validator_description: Reduced attenuation intracardiac blood left ventricle — associated with anemia
management_impact: Clinical correlation for anemia prompted; no specific CT follow-up required
notes: Doctor omits anemia sign. Prompts clinical workup for anemia.

validator_description: Severe CAC noted (PCP referral not explicit in impression)
management_impact: None — both document severe CAC; ACRad44 satisfied by doctor

validator_description: Decreased vertebral body density: T11 85 HU, T12 80 HU — consistent with osteoporosis
management_impact: None — CT finding, clinical correlation warranted
notes: Doctor omits vertebral body density values and osteoporosis finding.

validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes of shoulder joints
management_impact: None — incidental finding on chest CT
notes: Doctor omits shoulder joint findings.

doctor_description: Mild GGOs right lower lobe (unchanged) — likely hypoventilatory in nature
validator_description: Subpleural mild GGOs left upper lobe (unchanged) — likely interstitial changes
management_impact: Interstitial characterization implies different etiology than hypoventilatory; stable status limits urgency. No immediate management change.
notes: Different location (RLL vs LUL) AND different characterization (hypoventilatory vs interstitial). Both note unchanged GGOs but clinical framing differs.

doctor_description: Solid 6mm nodule, unchanged — if risk factors CT 12 months; per Fleischner no follow-up if no risk factors
validator_description: 6mm nodule — likely perifissural node, no follow-up required
management_impact: Doctor leaves conditional CT 12m option open; validator definitively states no follow-up for perifissural node. May result in unnecessary CT 12m.
notes: Validator correctly identifies as likely perifissural node requiring no follow-up. Doctor's conditional recommendation may trigger unnecessary CT. Measure 364 — validator's definitive no-follow-up vs doctor's ambiguous conditional.

doctor_description: Moderate CAC noted in findings; impression: 'Moderate CAC. Pacemaker.' — no explicit PCP referral
validator_description: Moderate CAC + PCP referral explicitly stated
management_impact: Doctor omits PCP referral from impression — ACRad44 not fully satisfied; patient may not receive cardiovascular risk referral
notes: Doctor documents CAC but fails to include PCP referral recommendation in impression. ACRad44 MIPS violation.

doctor_description: Pericardial fluid 1.2cm thick
validator_description: Pericardial fluid 7mm
management_impact: None — both describe small effusion; no actionable management difference
notes: Large measurement discrepancy (12mm vs 7mm). Both describe small stable effusion.

doctor_description: Hypodense lesions + bile duct dilation + decreased segment 4 density — MRI recommended in near term per ACR White Paper
validator_description: Cystic liver lesions — per ACR White Paper low risk group: no further follow-up
management_impact: Doctor over-recommends MRI for what validator characterizes as simple cysts. Unnecessary imaging workup triggered.
notes: Doctor groups liver cysts with bile duct dilation creating combined concern; validator separates and correctly applies Measure 405 for cysts. Doctor's MRI recommendation may be unnecessary for cysts alone.

doctor_description: Heterogeneous thyroid with hypodense lesions up to 1.2×1.2cm — thyroid US recommended per ACR White Paper 2014
validator_description: Hypodense lesions up to 6mm — no further follow-up per ACR White Paper
management_impact: If true size is 6mm, doctor's US recommendation is incorrect per ACR — Measure 406 violation. If true size is 1.2cm, US is correctly recommended. Size measurement discrepancy is root cause.
notes: Major size discrepancy (1.2cm vs 6mm). If lesion truly <1cm, Measure 406 MIPS violation by doctor (recommends US for sub-threshold nodule).

doctor_description: Healing fractures posterior segments right 5th, 6th, 7th ribs at necks
management_impact: None — stable healing rib fractures; no specific follow-up triggered
notes: Doctor documents healing rib fractures; validator omits. Doctor is more thorough; no management consequence.

management_impact: None — stable unchanged finding
